source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant (Customer Care Focus) [South Africa]
source_jobs.description:
South Africa (100% Remote) · Full-Time Independent Contractor ·

Be the calm, capable voice on the other end of every freight call. You'll keep customers updated, coordinate with drivers and dispatch, and make sure every load is tracked, communicated, and on time.

ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll serve as the primary point of contact between a U.S.-based asset-based trucking and logistics company and its shippers and clients. You'll handle quote and booking requests, real-time tracking updates, delivery ETAs, and the occasional curveball — a delay, a missed pickup, a customer who needs reassurance.

You'll work inside a Transportation Management System (TMS), coordinate with drivers and internal operations, and support the brokerage team on last-minute, time-sensitive shipments. The pace is fast, the inbox is full, and customers expect real updates — not generic ones. If you can stay organized when ten trucks are moving and three customers are calling, you'll thrive here.

The Ideal Candidate

 - You stay calm under pressure — a late truck, a tight ETA, and a frustrated shipper all get the same steady voice

 - You're proactive — customers hear from you before they have to ask

 - You can keep ten loads, three drivers, and five customers straight in your head and in your TMS

 - You write clear updates that don't bury the actual answer

 - You believe reliability and safety aren't slogans — they're how you work

WHAT YOU'LL OWN

Customer Care & Communication

Be the voice clients trust on every load.

 - Receive and process freight quote and booking requests

 - Provide real-time shipment tracking updates and accurate ETAs

 - Handle service issues, delays, and complaints with a calm, professional tone

 - Escalate appropriately when issues require operations or leadership input

Dispatch Coordination

Keep trucks, drivers, and customers in sync.

 - Coordinate with drivers and internal operations on pickups, deliveries, and route updates

 - Assist with load entry, documentation, and TMS updates

 - Support the brokerage division with last-minute or time-sensitive shipment coordination

Systems & Tools

Work cleanly in the tools that move freight.

 - Use the company's TMS to track shipments, manage resources, and generate reports

 - Stay sharp on email, phone, and chat communication tools

 - Keep records and documentation organized and accessible

Industry Fluency

Speak the language clients expect.

 - Communicate confidently about freight types (Dry Van, Reefer, Flatbed, LTL, etc.)

 - Understand basic logistics workflows: pickup → transit → delivery → POD

 - Bring a safety-first, reliability-first mindset to every interaction

WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - High school diploma or equivalent (industry training or coursework a plus)

 - 1–3 years of experience in customer service, logistics, dispatch, or supply chain

 - Familiarity with a Transportation Management System (TMS) or similar logistics platform

 - Excellent verbal and written English; client-facing presence

 - Strong attention to detail across multiple trucks, customers, and routes

 - Ability to multitask and stay organized under pressure

 - Proficiency with email, phone, and chat communication tools

 - Comfortable working U.S. business hours

Preferred (nice to have):

 - Prior experience in freight, trucking, or a similar fast-paced logistics environment

 - Knowledge of freight types and standard industry terminology

 - Experience supporting a brokerage or dispatch team

HOW WE WORK

 - Quality: Clear updates, accurate TMS entries, and tidy documentation — every load

 - Security: MFA, least-privilege access, and secure file sharing are non-negotiable

 - Collaboration: Slack/Zoom/Teams standups, shared task boards, and predictable check-ins

WHY CAS IS DIFFERENT

We are not a gig platform. We are not a freelance marketplace. We are not short-term staffing.

We invest in professionals who want to build a real career serving U.S. clients. At CAS, you get:

 - Career-l

source_jobs.title: US Accountant (Latin America)
source_jobs.description:
Latin America (100% Remote) · Full-Time Independent Contractor · $1,600–$3,500 USD/month ·

Imagine owning a clean, predictable close — every month. Your checklists sharp, your reconciliations airtight, your reports telling a story leaders can act on. That’s this role.



ABOUT THE ROLE

You’ll support fast-growing U.S. companies and CPA firms as a Latin America-based US Accountant — driving both accuracy and clarity. Full-time IC with exclusivity. No other engagements after hire.



You see accounting as a system: inputs tidy, flows efficient, outputs decision-ready. You’ll own the close, stitch together AP/AR, payroll, and reconciliations, then ship reporting that blends variance insight with plain-English commentary. Along the way you’ll standardize checklists, codify playbooks, and turn “oops” into automation.



If you like turning chaos into order and insights into action — welcome.



THE IDEAL CANDIDATE



 - You’ve closed books for U.S. entities and can hit the ground running with minimal supervision

 - You balance precision with practicality — you know when to dive deep and when to move fast

 - You enjoy creating order (SOPs, templates, checklists) and love clean ledgers and tidy reconciliations

 - You’re tech-curious and look for ways to automate routine work and reduce errors

 - You build trust with clients through consistent delivery, transparency, and simple explanations
   
   

WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Financial Reporting

Your reports tell a story leaders can act on.

 - Prepare timely balance sheets, P&Ls, and cash-flow statements with variance analysis and plain-English insights

 - Build budgets, rolling forecasts, and cash-flow projections; monitor burn and runway for SMB clients

 - Join standing client calls, translate financials into plain English, and proactively flag risks and opportunities

Books & Reconciliations

Precision is your baseline, not your ceiling.

 - Manage the books end-to-end: AP/AR, month-end close, journal entries, accruals, and the general ledger

 - Reconcile bank, credit card, merchant, and loan accounts; investigate and clear discrepancies

 - Run payroll accurately: calculate wages, deductions, and withholdings; coordinate approvals; ensure on-time processing

 - Maintain organized, audit-ready files and strong documentation across periods and entities

Process & Systems

Build the playbooks. Be the one who turns chaos into clarity.

 - Standardize checklists, close calendars, and SOPs; suggest tech automations (rules, workflows, integrations)

 - Maintain data quality and controls — every file clean, every period documented, every record audit-ready

 - 

WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline

 - 2+ years of U.S. accounting/bookkeeping experience (public practice or industry)

 - QuickBooks Online proficiency — required, with clean documented workflows

 - Strong English communication — written, spoken, and client-facing; service-oriented mindset

 - Experienced CPA or non-CPA — both are welcome here

 - Ownership mentality: organized, deadline-driven, managing multiple clients without sacrificing accuracy
   
   

Preferred (nice to have):

 - Xero, Bill.com http://Bill.com (or similar AP/AR automation)

 - G-Suite/Excel (pivot tables, lookups), and basic reporting/BI tools
   
   

HOW WE WORK

Quality: Close checklists, review notes, and documentation are part of every engagement

Security: Best practices for data privacy, MFA, and least-privilege access — always

Collaboration: Slack/Zoom standups, shared task boards, and predictable review cycles



WHY CAS IS DIFFERENT

We are not a gig platform. We are not a freelance marketplace. We are not short-term staffing.

We invest in professionals who want to build a real career serving U.S. clients. At CAS, you get:

 - Career-level USD compensation: $1,600–$3,500 USD/month based on experience and portfolio complexity

 - Permanent remote work — done right

 - Full company equipment

source_jobs.title: US Tax Accountant (Latin America)
source_jobs.description:
Location: Latin America (100% Remote)

Employment Type: Full-time Independent Contractor (exclusive commitment)

Compensation: $2,100–$3,800 USD per month (commensurate with experience and skills)




ABOUT THE JOB

Be the expert clients trust at the moment it matters most—tax time. As a US Tax Accountant (or Tax Manager, depending on experience), you’ll prepare and review complex returns, lead smart tax planning, and turn dense regulations into practical guidance. You’ll collaborate with US-based teams, mentor junior staff, and help modernize how small businesses and CPA firms handle compliance and advisory.

Note: This is a full-time independent contractor role. Exclusivity is required—no part-time or freelance work once hired.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You’ll own the work that keeps leaders calm and compliant: multi-entity 1040/1065/1120/1120-S preparation & review, clear planning memos, airtight workpapers, and timely sign-offs. You’ll spot elections, credits, and apportionment issues before they become problems, then explain them in plain English. You’ll sharpen playbooks—checklists, review notes, and close calendars—so every busy season feels a little less busy.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Deep-Dive: Tax Preparation & Review

 - Own tax intake end-to-end: Drive organizers, smart document requests, and kickoff calls; map client TBs and prior-year returns to this year’s workplan.

 - Entity expertise (1065/1120/1120-S):
   
   - Build and review K-1s, capital accounts, partner/shareholder basis and at-risk
   
   - Evaluate §199A (QBI) eligibility and limitations; document wage/UBIA computations.
   
   - Assess reasonable compensation for S-corp shareholder-employees.

 - Individual returns (1040):
   
   - Prepare clean Schedules A/B/C/D/E, Form 4797, 6252 (installment sales), passive activity loss and basis tracking, 1099-B reconciliations, wash sale checks.
   
   - Roll forward carryforwards (NOL, capital loss, AMT credits) and manage ES payments/safe harbors.

 - Multi-state & SALT: Calculate apportionment (sales/payroll/property), manage nexus updates, composite/non-resident returns, and PTE/SALT cap workaround elections where applicable.

 - Credits & elections: Surface and document opportunities (R&D for eligible filers, 179/bonus choices, accounting method changes, late elections relief memos).

 - Fixed assets & depreciation: Maintain Form 4562 schedules, asset ledgers, and disposal/partial disposition rules; reconcile book vs tax depreciation.

 - Quality & sign-off: Build audit-ready workpapers with clear tick marks, review notes, and support; manage e-file submissions, acknowledgments, and extensions.

 - Notices & follow-through: Draft concise responses to IRS/state notices, set reminders for follow-ups, and keep clients calm with clear options and timelines.

 - Calendar discipline: Own busy-season checkpoints (intake → prep → review → client Q&A → e-file) and off-season planning (estimates, projections, method changes).

Client Collaboration

 - Join standing calls, summarize impacts (cash, rate, timing), and deliver one-page takeaways leaders can act on. Translate code citations into business decisions.

Workflow & Process Improvement

 - Standardize SOPs, checklists, and review gates; set up rules/automations in tax and accounting tools to reduce manual steps; capture wins in a living playbook.

Data Integrity

 - Keep folders organized, naming consistent, and documentation complete so any reviewer (or auditor) can follow your trail quickly.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline

 - 3+ years US Tax experience preparing and reviewing 1040, 1065, 1120, 1120-S.

 - Strong command of US federal & state tax rules; comfort with GAAP and IRS procedures.

 - Tax Software Proficiency: ProConnect, UltraTax, Drake, Lacerte (or similar).

 - Cloud-first toolkit: QuickBooks Online, Xero; strong Excel/Google Sheets skills (lookups, pivots).

 - Communication: Confident written/s

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant (Philippines)
source_jobs.description:
Location: Philippines (100% Remote)

Employment Type: Full-time Independent Contractor (exclusive commitment)

Compensation: $1,000–$1,500 USD per month (commensurate with experience & scope)




ABOUT THE JOB

You’re the force that turns good teams into great ones. As an Executive Assistant at Cloud Accountant Staffing, you’ll orchestrate schedules, tame inboxes, streamline workflows, and make complex days feel simple. From client communications and research, to light bookkeeping and reporting, you’ll bring order, clarity, and momentum—so leaders can focus on the work only they can do. Full-time IC with exclusivity (no other part-time or freelance engagements once hired). If you love structure, crisp communication, and getting things done, you’ll feel right at home.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Think strategist-meets-executor. You’ll anticipate needs, reduce noise, and keep everything moving—meetings prepped, follow-ups sent, docs clean, tasks tracked, and outcomes documented. You’ll translate open-ended requests into a structured checklist, choose the right tool (CRM, doc, board, or template), and close loops with friendly, professional updates. Calm under pressure, precise in your writing, and proactive with solutions—that’s your signature.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Calendar & Inbox Command: Manage busy executive calendars, triage emails, draft replies, schedule across US time zones, and protect focus time.

 - Client Communications: Respond to inquiries, prep meeting agendas, capture minutes/action items, and own follow-ups to completion.

 - Project Support: Build task boards, track deadlines, and maintain status updates in tools like Asana/ClickUp/Trello; escalate risks early.

 - Documentation & SOPs: Create and maintain checklists, templates, and SOPs so recurring work becomes predictable and faster.

 - Research & Reporting: Conduct targeted research (vendors, tools, market info), synthesize findings, and prepare concise summaries or slide decks.

 - Data & Files: Clean up docs and folders, maintain naming/versioning standards, and ensure everything’s easy to find.

 - Light Bookkeeping (as needed): Categorize expenses, match receipts, prep simple invoices/credit notes, and coordinate with the accounting team (QuickBooks/Expensify basics).

 - CRM Hygiene: Update records, log activities, prep lists/exports, and keep pipelines tidy (HubSpot/Salesforce or similar).

 - Travel & Logistics: Arrange travel, prepare itineraries, and manage event logistics when required.

 - Quality, Privacy & Security: Follow data-handling policies, use MFA, and observe least-privilege access at all times.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 3+ years in a Virtual Assistant/Executive Assistant/Operations role supporting C-level leaders or Senior stakeholders.

 - Tool fluency: Google Workspace (Docs/Sheets/Drive), Microsoft Office, Slack/Zoom/Teams, and at least one CRM.

 - Organization & Follow-through: You live by checklists, time blocking, and clear documentation; nothing falls through the cracks.

 - Excellent English (written & spoken): clear, friendly, and professional—your emails read like you mean business.

 - Problem solving: You turn vague asks into concrete next steps; you propose options, not obstacles.

 - Time-zone alignment: Comfortable working US business hours

 - Nice-to-haves: Light bookkeeping, basic reporting/BI know-how, experience with Notion, Calendly, Zapier, Canva/Slides.




THE IDEAL CANDIDATE

 - Predictive, not reactive: You anticipate needs and prepare materials before they’re asked for.

 - Detail-obsessed, deadline-driven: You spot inconsistencies, fix formatting, and hit send with confidence.

 - Process-minded: You love creating templates/SOPs and shaving minutes off recurring tasks with smart shortcuts.

 - Calm communicator: You keep stakeholders aligned with brief, timely updates and well-labeled docs.

 - Team-first: You collaborate openly, ask crisp questions, and share what you’ve standardized so others benefit.

You’ll collaborate with US-based teams, mentor junior staff, and help modernize how small businesses and CPA firms handle compliance and advisory.

Note: This is a full-time independent contractor role. Exclusivity is required—no part-time or freelance work once hired.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You’ll own the work that keeps leaders calm and compliant: multi-entity 1040/1065/1120/1120-S preparation & review, clear planning memos, airtight workpapers, and timely sign-offs. You’ll spot elections, credits, and apportionment issues before they become problems, then explain them in plain English. You’ll sharpen playbooks—checklists, review notes, and close calendars—so every busy season feels a little less busy.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Deep-Dive: Tax Preparation & Review

 - Own tax intake end-to-end: Drive organizers, smart document requests, and kickoff calls; map client TBs and prior-year returns to this year’s workplan.

 - Entity expertise (1065/1120/1120-S):
   
   - Build and review K-1s, capital accounts, partner/shareholder basis and at-risk
   
   - Evaluate §199A (QBI) eligibility and limitations; document wage/UBIA computations.
   
   - Assess reasonable compensation for S-corp shareholder-employees.

 - Individual returns (1040):
   
   - Prepare clean Schedules A/B/C/D/E, Form 4797, 6252 (installment sales), passive activity loss and basis tracking, 1099-B reconciliations, wash sale checks.
   
   - Roll forward carryforwards (NOL, capital loss, AMT credits) and manage ES payments/safe harbors.

 - Multi-state & SALT: Calculate apportionment (sales/payroll/property), manage nexus updates, composite/non-resident returns, and PTE/SALT cap workaround elections where applicable.

 - Credits & elections: Surface and document opportunities (R&D for eligible filers, 179/bonus choices, accounting method changes, late elections relief memos).

 - Fixed assets & depreciation: Maintain Form 4562 schedules, asset ledgers, and disposal/partial disposition rules; reconcile book vs tax depreciation.

 - Quality & sign-off: Build audit-ready workpapers with clear tick marks, review notes, and support; manage e-file submissions, acknowledgments, and extensions.

 - Notices & follow-through: Draft concise responses to IRS/state notices, set reminders for follow-ups, and keep clients calm with clear options and timelines.

 - Calendar discipline: Own busy-season checkpoints (intake → prep → review → client Q&A → e-file) and off-season planning (estimates, projections, method changes).

Client Collaboration

 - Join standing calls, summarize impacts (cash, rate, timing), and deliver one-page takeaways leaders can act on. Translate code citations into business decisions.

Workflow & Process Improvement

 - Standardize SOPs, checklists, and review gates; set up rules/automations in tax and accounting tools to reduce manual steps; capture wins in a living playbook.

Data Integrity

 - Keep folders organized, naming consistent, and documentation complete so any reviewer (or auditor) can follow your trail quickly.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline

 - 3+ years US Tax experience preparing and reviewing 1040, 1065, 1120, 1120-S.

 - Strong command of US federal & state tax rules; comfort with GAAP and IRS procedures.

 - Tax Software Proficiency: ProConnect, UltraTax, Drake, Lacerte (or similar).

 - Cloud-first toolkit: QuickBooks Online, Xero; strong Excel/Google Sheets skills (lookups, pivots).

 - Communication: Confident written/sp

Compensation: $700–$1,200 USD per month (commensurate with experience & scope)




ABOUT THE JOB

You’re the force that turns good teams into great ones. As an Executive Assistant at Cloud Accountant Staffing, you’ll orchestrate schedules, tame inboxes, streamline workflows, and make complex days feel simple. From client communications and research, to light bookkeeping and reporting, you’ll bring order, clarity, and momentum—so leaders can focus on the work only they can do. Full-time IC with exclusivity (no other part-time or freelance engagements once hired). If you love structure, crisp communication, and getting things done, you’ll feel right at home.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Think strategist-meets-executor. You’ll anticipate needs, reduce noise, and keep everything moving—meetings prepped, follow-ups sent, docs clean, tasks tracked, and outcomes documented. You’ll translate open-ended requests into a structured checklist, choose the right tool (CRM, doc, board, or template), and close loops with friendly, professional updates. Calm under pressure, precise in your writing, and proactive with solutions—that’s your signature.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Calendar & Inbox Command: Manage busy executive calendars, triage emails, draft replies, schedule across US time zones, and protect focus time.

 - Client Communications: Respond to inquiries, prep meeting agendas, capture minutes/action items, and own follow-ups to completion.

 - Project Support: Build task boards, track deadlines, and maintain status updates in tools like Asana/ClickUp/Trello; escalate risks early.

 - Documentation & SOPs: Create and maintain checklists, templates, and SOPs so recurring work becomes predictable and faster.

 - Research & Reporting: Conduct targeted research (vendors, tools, market info), synthesize findings, and prepare concise summaries or slide decks.

 - Data & Files: Clean up docs and folders, maintain naming/versioning standards, and ensure everything’s easy to find.

 - Light Bookkeeping (as needed): Categorize expenses, match receipts, prep simple invoices/credit notes, and coordinate with the accounting team (QuickBooks/Expensify basics).

 - CRM Hygiene: Update records, log activities, prep lists/exports, and keep pipelines tidy (HubSpot/Salesforce or similar).

 - Travel & Logistics: Arrange travel, prepare itineraries, and manage event logistics when required.

 - Quality, Privacy & Security: Follow data-handling policies, use MFA, and observe least-privilege access at all times.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 3+ years in a Virtual Assistant/Executive Assistant/Operations role supporting C-level leaders or Senior stakeholders.

 - Tool fluency: Google Workspace (Docs/Sheets/Drive), Microsoft Office, Slack/Zoom/Teams, and at least one CRM.

 - Organization & Follow-through: You live by checklists, time blocking, and clear documentation; nothing falls through the cracks.

 - Excellent English (written & spoken): clear, friendly, and professional—your emails read like you mean business.

 - Problem solving: You turn vague asks into concrete next steps; you propose options, not obstacles.

 - Time-zone alignment: Comfortable working US business hours

 - Nice-to-haves: Light bookkeeping, basic reporting/BI know-how, experience with Notion, Calendly, Zapier, Canva/Slides.




THE IDEAL CANDIDATE

 - Predictive, not reactive: You anticipate needs and prepare materials before they’re asked for.

 - Detail-obsessed, deadline-driven: You spot inconsistencies, fix formatting, and hit send with confidence.

 - Process-minded: You love creating templates/SOPs and shaving minutes off recurring tasks with smart shortcuts.

 - Calm communicator: You keep stakeholders aligned with brief, timely updates and well-labeled docs.

 - Team-first: You collaborate openly, ask crisp questions, and share what you’ve standardized so others benefit.

Help build world-class accounting departments for growing US companies. As a Controller, you will oversee accounting operations, lead a team, strengthen GAAP compliance, drive month-end accuracy, and ensure executives receive clean, timely financial data they can trust.

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant (Customer Care Focus) [Philippines]
source_jobs.description:
Philippines (100% Remote) · Full-Time Independent Contractor ·

Be the calm, capable voice on the other end of every freight call. You'll keep customers updated, coordinate with drivers and dispatch, and make sure every load is tracked, communicated, and on time.

ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll serve as the primary point of contact between a U.S.-based asset-based trucking and logistics company and its shippers and clients. You'll handle quote and booking requests, real-time tracking updates, delivery ETAs, and the occasional curveball — a delay, a missed pickup, a customer who needs reassurance.

You'll work inside a Transportation Management System (TMS), coordinate with drivers and internal operations, and support the brokerage team on last-minute, time-sensitive shipments. The pace is fast, the inbox is full, and customers expect real updates — not generic ones. If you can stay organized when ten trucks are moving and three customers are calling, you'll thrive here.

The Ideal Candidate

 - You stay calm under pressure — a late truck, a tight ETA, and a frustrated shipper all get the same steady voice

 - You're proactive — customers hear from you before they have to ask

 - You can keep ten loads, three drivers, and five customers straight in your head and in your TMS

 - You write clear updates that don't bury the actual answer

 - You believe reliability and safety aren't slogans — they're how you work

WHAT YOU'LL OWN

Customer Care & Communication

Be the voice clients trust on every load.

 - Receive and process freight quote and booking requests

 - Provide real-time shipment tracking updates and accurate ETAs

 - Handle service issues, delays, and complaints with a calm, professional tone

 - Escalate appropriately when issues require operations or leadership input

Dispatch Coordination

Keep trucks, drivers, and customers in sync.

 - Coordinate with drivers and internal operations on pickups, deliveries, and route updates

 - Assist with load entry, documentation, and TMS updates

 - Support the brokerage division with last-minute or time-sensitive shipment coordination

Systems & Tools

Work cleanly in the tools that move freight.

 - Use the company's TMS to track shipments, manage resources, and generate reports

 - Stay sharp on email, phone, and chat communication tools

 - Keep records and documentation organized and accessible

Industry Fluency

Speak the language clients expect.

 - Communicate confidently about freight types (Dry Van, Reefer, Flatbed, LTL, etc.)

 - Understand basic logistics workflows: pickup → transit → delivery → POD

 - Bring a safety-first, reliability-first mindset to every interaction

WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - High school diploma or equivalent (industry training or coursework a plus)

 - 1–3 years of experience in customer service, logistics, dispatch, or supply chain

 - Familiarity with a Transportation Management System (TMS) or similar logistics platform

 - Excellent verbal and written English; client-facing presence

 - Strong attention to detail across multiple trucks, customers, and routes

 - Ability to multitask and stay organized under pressure

 - Proficiency with email, phone, and chat communication tools

 - Comfortable working U.S. business hours (night shift, PH-based)

Preferred (nice to have):

 - Prior experience in freight, trucking, or a similar fast-paced logistics environment

 - Knowledge of freight types and standard industry terminology

 - Experience supporting a brokerage or dispatch team

HOW WE WORK

 - Quality: Clear updates, accurate TMS entries, and tidy documentation — every load

 - Security: MFA, least-privilege access, and secure file sharing are non-negotiable

 - Collaboration: Slack/Zoom/Teams standups, shared task boards, and predictable check-ins

WHY CAS IS DIFFERENT

We are not a gig platform. We are not a freelance marketplace. We are not short-term staffing.

We invest in professionals who want to build a real career serving U.S. clients. At CAS

source_jobs.description:
Location: Philippines (100% Remote)

Employment Type: Full-time Independent Contractor (exclusive commitment)

Compensation: $1200-$1500 USD per month (commensurate with experience and skills)

About the Job

The Marketing Specialist supports and executes candidate-facing and sales-facing marketing initiatives that directly impact hiring, brand visibility, and revenue growth at Cloud Accountant Staffing (CAS).

This is a hands-on, execution-focused role for someone who enjoys variety, takes pride in delivering clean work, and wants to see their output put to use quickly. You’ll work across content, social media, and basic campaigns, partnering closely with Leadership, Talent, and Growth teams.

This role also includes direct interaction with US-based clients and stakeholders when needed. While the majority of the work is execution-focused, you will occasionally participate in meetings, provide updates, and communicate marketing progress professionally.

Clear direction is provided — success comes from ownership, consistency, follow-through, and clear communication.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You’ll help turn ideas and priorities into published content, scheduled posts, and usable marketing assets.

This role requires strong organization, attention to detail, and comfort working independently. While collaboration is a big part of the job, you’ll be trusted to manage your tasks, meet deadlines, and keep work moving without constant oversight.

In addition to internal collaboration, you may be asked to join client calls to provide marketing updates, clarify campaign direction, or discuss deliverables.

There may be a learning curve as you adapt to US communication standards and expectations. We are looking for someone who is coachable, proactive, and comfortable growing into client interactions over time.

If you enjoy doing meaningful work, staying organized, and supporting multiple teams through marketing execution — while gradually expanding your communication exposure — this role is a strong fit.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


CONTENT CREATION & WRITING

Create written content across multiple channels, including:

 - LinkedIn articles (weekly or monthly)

 - Social media captions and short-form posts

 - Candidate spotlights and success stories

 - Monthly staffer newsletters

 - Internal and external marketing announcements

Ensure all content aligns with CAS brand voice, tone, and business goals.
Edit and refine content for clarity, consistency, and readability.


SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGEMENT

 - Plan and execute social media content plans.

 - Create, schedule, and publish posts across approved platforms.

 - Maintain content calendars and posting consistency.

 - Monitor engagement and flag opportunities for improvement based on performance.


MEDIA & ASSET CREATION

Create on-brand visuals using tools like Canva (or similar), including:

 - Social graphics

 - Simple videos or motion assets

 - You stay on top of an inbox without letting anything slip

 - You learn new systems quickly and aren't afraid to ask questions

 - You take ownership of your queue, not just your tasks
   
   

WHAT YOU'LL OWN

AP Inbox & Communication

Data Entry & TMS Accuracy

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant: Marketing & Communications Lead (Philippines)
source_jobs.description:
At Cloud Accountant Staffing (CAS), we don’t build short-term roles.
We build long-term careers for professionals who want stability, growth, and meaningful work with global standards.



In this role, you step in as more than an assistant.

 - You become the operator behind communication, marketing, and day-to-day execution — the person who brings clarity, structure, and consistency across everything the business touches.

 - You’ll work closely with leadership, support client experience end-to-end, and ensure that both communication and marketing aren’t just running — but running well.

If you’re someone who naturally creates order, communicates with intention, and takes pride in making things work seamlessly — this is a role where that strength becomes your career advantage.



What you’ll take ownership of

This role sits at the intersection of communication, marketing, and operations.
You’re the person who keeps everything clear, consistent, and moving forward.

Client Communications & Operations — You’re the voice and the flow

 - You’ll be the steady presence behind every client interaction.
   From managing shared inboxes (hello@, care@, accounting@, tax@) to guiding clients through onboarding and offboarding, you ensure every message is clear, timely, and aligned.

 - You don’t just respond — you create structure.
   You build SOPs, refine templates, and design workflows that make things easier not just for today, but for the long run.

 - You also keep everything connected — aligning schedules, tracking details, and making sure nothing slips through the cracks.

Marketing Execution — You turn ideas into consistent output

 - You take ownership of the content calendar and make sure it actually happens.
   Not just planned — executed.

 - You schedule, publish, and keep things moving using tools like Social Pilot.
   You transform long-form ideas into structured, engaging content that people can actually consume.

 - And you don’t just “post and forget.”
   You pay attention to performance, identify what’s working, and continuously improve from there.

Systems & Leadership Support — You bring order to growth

 - You naturally think in systems.
   When something feels manual, unclear, or inefficient — you find a better way.

 - You introduce structure into communication and marketing workflows, and look for opportunities to improve through automation, AI tools, or smarter processes.

Most importantly, you act as a trusted right hand to leadership — helping turn direction into execution, and ensuring things don’t just start, but actually get finished.



What we’re looking for

We’re looking for someone who sees this role not as “support,” but as ownership.

• Strong experience managing client communications end-to-end
• Hands-on experience with onboarding/offboarding and process documentation
• Proven ability to manage and execute content calendars
• Clear, professional, and thoughtful written communication
• Experience building SOPs, templates, and structured workflows
• Highly organized, detail-oriented, and systems-driven
• Comfortable using Google Workspace (experience with Canopy or Social Pilot is a plus)
• Ability to work independently, take initiative, and follow through



More than anything, we’re looking for someone reliable, thoughtful, and consistent — the kind of person teams trust to keep things running at a high standard.



What makes CAS different

We’re not a freelance marketplace.
We’re not built on short-term work.
We build careers.

At CAS, you step into a role designed for long-term growth, stability, and real ownership:

• Competitive, career-level compensation
• Permanent work-from-home setup — done right
• Clear scope, structure, and expectations
• High-trust environment where your work matters
• Direct exposure to global teams and U.S. clients
• A culture grounded in clarity, growth, and continuous improvement



This is a role where your work directly shapes client experience, strengthens ope

source_jobs.title: US Tax Reviewer (Latin America)
source_jobs.description:
Latin America (100% Remote) · Full-Time Independent Contractor ·

Be the final set of eyes that says “this is ready to file.” You review completed returns across all major entity types, catch what others miss, and own the accuracy of every return that goes out the door.



ABOUT THE ROLE

You’ll serve as the final checkpoint in the tax workflow — the reviewer of record. Preparers hand off completed 1040s, 1120s, 1120-S, and 1065s; you make sure they’re right before they’re filed. You’ll spot calculation errors, missed elections, and documentation gaps, then coordinate with the preparer to get them resolved.

This is a higher-accountability role than preparation, and the compensation reflects it. You’ll communicate directly with clients — but strictly to clarify questions about the return itself. No advisory services. No planning conversations. Just clean, accurate returns and clear answers about what’s on them.

You’ll work in Drake and a proprietary internal platform (training provided), partner with U.S.-based teams on technical questions, and help raise the quality bar across the workflow.



The Ideal Candidate

 - You’re the person other reviewers double-check their work against

 - You can spot a missing K-1 footnote, a wrong basis calculation, or a miscoded depreciation method on the first pass

 - You stay in your lane on client calls — you answer what’s on the return, not what could be

 - You’re comfortable being the last name on the file

 - You believe a clean review note saves three rounds of rework
   
   

WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Return Review & Sign-Off

Be the final checkpoint before filing.

 - Review completed returns across 1040, 1120, 1120-S, and 1065 — federal and multi-state

 - Verify K-1s, capital accounts, basis, at-risk, §199A treatment, and reasonable comp where applicable

 - Reconcile book-to-tax adjustments, depreciation schedules, and carryforwards (NOL, capital loss, AMT, PALs)

 - Validate apportionment, nexus, composite/PTE elections, and state-specific quirks

 - Confirm e-file readiness: forms, signatures, acknowledgments, and extensions

Quality Control & Issue Resolution

Catch it before it ships.

 - Identify gaps, errors, or unclear treatment in prepared returns

 - Write clear, actionable review notes preparers can resolve without back-and-forth

 - Coordinate with the preparer through resolution; verify fixes before final sign-off

 - Escalate technical questions to internal U.S.-based colleagues when needed

Client Communication (Return-Scoped Only)

Answer what’s on the return — nothing more.

 - Respond to client questions specifically about line items, calculations, or filings on the prepared return

 - Explain results clearly without offering advisory or planning recommendations

 - Redirect advisory questions to the appropriate party

Tools & Workflow

Work cleanly in the tools that ship returns.

 - Drake tax software (preferred experience)

 - Proprietary internal platform — training provided

 - Maintain audit-ready workpapers and documentation trails

Data Integrity

Leave a trail anyone can follow.

 - Keep folders organized, naming consistent, and documentation complete so any reviewer or auditor can follow your work quickly
   
   

WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Extensive experience reviewing and/or preparing U.S. tax returns across 1040, 1120, 1120-S, and 1065

 - Deep familiarity with state tax filings across multiple jurisdictions

 - High degree of accuracy and professional judgment; comfortable taking final accountability for filed returns

 - Strong communication skills for both client-facing (return-specific) and internal correspondence

 - Experience with Drake tax software preferred

 - Confident written and spoken English; client-facing presence
   
   

Preferred (nice to have):

 - CPA or Enrolled Agent (EA) designation strongly preferred

 - Experience with R&D tax credits (Form 6765)

 - Familiarity with NYC local tax filings

 - Prior review or supervisory tax role

 - You stay in your lane on client calls — you answer what’s on the return, not what could be

 - You’re comfortable being the last name on the file

 - You believe a clean review note saves three rounds of rework

WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Return Review & Sign-Off

Be the final checkpoint before filing.

 - Review completed returns across 1040, 1120, 1120-S, and 1065 — federal and multi-state

 - Verify K-1s, capital accounts, basis, at-risk, §199A treatment, and reasonable comp where applicable

 - Reconcile book-to-tax adjustments, depreciation schedules, and carryforwards (NOL, capital loss, AMT, PALs)

 - Validate apportionment, nexus, composite/PTE elections, and state-specific quirks

 - Confirm e-file readiness: forms, signatures, acknowledgments, and extensions

Quality Control & Issue Resolution

Catch it before it ships.

 - Identify gaps, errors, or unclear treatment in prepared returns

 - Write clear, actionable review notes preparers can resolve without back-and-forth

 - Coordinate with the preparer through resolution; verify fixes before final sign-off

 - Escalate technical questions to internal U.S.-based colleagues when needed

Client Communication (Return-Scoped Only)

Answer what’s on the return — nothing more.

 - Respond to client questions specifically about line items, calculations, or filings on the prepared return

 - Explain results clearly without offering advisory or planning recommendations

 - Redirect advisory questions to the appropriate party

Tools & Workflow

Work cleanly in the tools that ship returns.

 - Drake tax software (preferred experience)

 - Proprietary internal platform — training provided

 - Maintain audit-ready workpapers and documentation trails

Data Integrity

Leave a trail anyone can follow.

 - Keep folders organized, naming consistent, and documentation complete so any reviewer or auditor can follow your work quickly

 - Extensive experience reviewing and/or preparing U.S. tax returns across 1040, 1120, 1120-S, and 1065

 - Deep familiarity with state tax filings across multiple jurisdictions

 - High degree of accuracy and professional judgment; comfortable taking final accountability for filed returns

 - Strong communication skills for both client-facing (return-specific) and internal correspondence

 - Experience with Drake tax software preferred

 - Confident written and spoken English; client-facing presence

 - Comfortable working U.S. business hours (night shift, PH-based)

 - CPA or Enrolled Agent (EA) designation strongly preferred

 - Experience with R&D tax credits (Form 6765)

Latin America (100% Remote) · Full-Time



Build the talent engine behind every great hire and every great retention story. You'll lead recruitment and staffer success end-to-end — attracting the right people, supporting them once they're here, and building the systems that make both possible at scale.



ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll head up CAS's Talent function, reporting to the Director of Operations and owning two halves of the same equation: bringing in the right people, and making sure they thrive once they're hired. You'll set the recruitment strategy, run the funnel, and build pipelines for the roles that come up again and again — then turn around and design the programs that help staffers grow, perform, and stay.



You'll lead the Talent team, partner with department heads on hiring plans, work with HR/Operations on the policies that shape a staffer's experience, and bring data discipline to all of it — tracking time-to-hire, retention, and performance trends, then using what you learn to keep tightening the system.



This is a high-impact leadership role for someone who's done recruitment and people success before and wants to own both at the strategic level.



The Ideal Candidate

 - You think in funnels and feedback loops — you don't just hire, you build the system that hires

 - You've sat on both sides: recruiting top talent and helping them succeed once they're in

 - You can move from a workforce-planning conversation to a 1:1 coaching moment without losing the thread

 - You're data-driven but human-first — metrics inform you, but people decisions still need judgment

 - You believe culture isn't a slogan; it's the sum of how leaders show up every day
   
   

WHAT YOU'LL OWN

Recruitment Strategy & Execution

Build the engine that brings in great people.

 - Develop and execute talent acquisition strategies that meet current and future staffing needs

 - Oversee the full recruitment lifecycle — job posting through onboarding — and own candidate experience

 - Build and maintain pipelines for critical and recurring roles

 - Set and track recruitment KPIs (time-to-hire, source quality, offer acceptance)
   
   

Staffer Success & Retention

Help the people we hire thrive.

 - Design and lead initiatives that help staffers excel and grow in their roles

 - Monitor performance, provide feedback, and design clear development pathways

 - Identify drivers of satisfaction, motivation, and retention — and act on them

 - Champion a culture aligned with company values and high performance
   
   

Team Leadership

Lead the Talent team.

 - Manage and develop the Talent team; set clear goals, provide mentorship, and grow the next leader

 - Partner with department heads to align hiring with workforce planning

 - Collaborate with HR/Operations to ensure policies and processes enable success
   
   

Measurement & Continuous Improvement

Lead with data; tune with judgment.

 - Track and report on time-to-hire, performance trends, and retention metrics

 - Continuously improve recruitment and staffer success processes based on data and feedback

 - Surface insights to the Director of Operations and broader leadership team
   
   

WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Proven experience in recruitment leadership and employee success/engagement leadership

 - Strong interpersonal and communication skills; able to influence across levels

 - Strategic mindset paired with hands-on execution

 - Ability to foster an inclusive, supportive, and high-performance work environment

 - Comfortable working with data — KPIs, dashboards, retention analyses

 - Experience leading and developing a team

 - Confident written and spoken English
   
   

Preferred (nice to have):

 - Background in staffing, outsourcing, or BPO environments

 - Familiarity with ATS platforms (e.g., Ashby, Greenhouse, Workable)

 - Experience scaling talent functions in a fast-growing company

source_jobs.title: Director of Finance (South Africa)
source_jobs.description:
South Africa (100% Remote) · Full-Time Independent Contractor



Be the analytical engine behind every great CFO decision. You'll own the FP&A execution, financial modeling, and KPI infrastructure that lets fractional CFOs focus on strategy — across a portfolio of high-growth consumer and retail brands.



ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll serve as the analytical and operational backbone of a pod-based delivery model — partnering with a Client CFO and a controllership/bookkeeping team to support multiple consumer and retail brands. You'll own the FP&A execution: three-statement models, rolling forecasts, KPI dashboards, board materials, and the analytical work that turns numbers into decisions.

Today, the CFOs you support spend roughly 70% of their time on reporting, analytics, and model maintenance. That's not the highest use of a CFO's time. You change that. You take ownership of the analytical engine so CFOs can operate at the level their clients deserve — strategic planning, capital allocation, and being a real thought partner to founders.

This role demands the technical depth of an investment banker, the commercial judgment to advise operators directly, and the professional maturity to step into client meetings independently when a CFO isn't available. You should be equally comfortable building a three-statement model from scratch and walking a VP of Supply Chain through the margin implications of a new vendor contract.



The Ideal Candidate

 - You build models the way an IB analyst builds models — clean, auditable, dynamic

 - You can read a P&L like a story and tell anyone in the room what's actually happening

 - You hold your own with founders, operators, and functional heads — finance person or not

 - You see recurring work as a systems problem, not a workload problem

 - You thrive in lean, high-performance environments where outcomes matter more than tenure
   
   

WHAT YOU'LL OWN

Financial Modeling, Forecasting & Reporting

Own the analytical engine.

 - Build and maintain dynamic 1/3/5-year financial models (three-statement, scenario analysis, sensitivity tables)

 - Own rolling forecasts, annual budgets, and variance analysis across a portfolio of clients

 - Develop and manage KPI dashboards, executive reporting packages, and board-ready materials

 - Drive margin analysis, demand planning, and unit economics reviews

 - Ensure data integrity by partnering with internal accounting and controllership teams on reconciliations and close outputs
   
   

CFO Pod Support & Client Coverage

Be the CFO's primary analytical partner.

 - Serve as the analytical lead to Client CFOs across multiple engagements

 - Prepare materials and analysis for board meetings, investor calls, and strategic planning sessions

 - Step into client meetings independently when needed — engaging directly with marketing, supply chain, operations, and other functional leaders

 - Provide decision support on commercial questions: pricing, channel mix, inventory planning, vendor negotiations

 - Translate complex financial data into clear, actionable insights for non-finance stakeholders
   
   

Capital Markets & Strategic Advisory Support

Show up for the deal moments.

 - Support fundraising and M&A: investor decks, data rooms, credit packs, due diligence materials

 - Assist with debt strategy, capital allocation scenarios, and cash runway planning

 - Build and maintain lender and investor reporting packages

 - Provide deal-time analytical support: LOI evaluation, term sheet comparison, closing mechanics
   
   

Workflow Optimization & Systems Thinking

Make every cycle faster than the last.

 - Templatize, automate, and scale recurring analytical workflows across engagements

 - Use Excel, Google Sheets, AI platforms (Claude, ChatGPT), and automation tools to deliver faster and smarter

 - Document SOPs and contribute to internal playbooks and the firm's knowledge base

 - Identify opportunities to improve reporting cadence, data pipelines,
